content_selection.rationale: The brief kept the freshest, highest-value July 6-7 stories (J-space interpretability, METR's record eval-gaming finding, the $19B TeraWulf lease, government adoption push) and correctly cut stale or soft candidates: the April Anthropic-revenue milestone, the June 26 GPT-5.6 base story (whose live angles it still covers via METR and the White House framework), and the consciousness-hiring piece that reporting confirms has no operational news peg. The main weakness is folding the record SK Hynix Nasdaq listing entirely into the Samsung stock-reaction item rather than giving one of history's largest share sales — pricing July 9, trading July 10 — its own slot. Otherwise the included items are all substantive and current, and the Anthropic-heavy tilt genuinely reflects the news cycle rather than editorial imbalance.
content_selection.evidence: SK Hynix would rank 'among the largest ever' share sales with 'Pricing is expected Thursday, July 9, with shares set to begin trading on Friday, July 10' — a major AI-driven event covered only obliquely inside the Samsung item, while the excluded Anthropic-revenue story was an April 2026 milestone (correctly cut).

selection_disagreements.story: SK Hynix ~$28B Nasdaq listing (base IPO story)
selection_disagreements.rationale: Multiple sources (Reuters, TheStreet, TechTimes) confirm this is one of the largest share sales in history and the largest-ever U.S. listing by a foreign company behind only SpaceX, is directly AI/HBM-demand driven, is oversubscribed with $7B in cornerstone interest, and is imminent (pricing July 9, trading July 10). The brief only references SK Hynix in passing within the Samsung reaction item; a listing of this scale and timeliness warranted its own short entry rather than being fully excluded.

factual_accuracy.rationale: I verified every headline and key factual claim across all sections against live, current sources including primary sources (TeraWulf SEC 8-K, METR's blog, Anthropic's own posts, the White House EO 14409 text) and multiple corroborating outlets. Every checked figure, date, product name, dollar amount, and quote matched what my research found, and the brief appropriately hedges the items it itself flags as unconfirmed (the White House framework timing) or weakly-sourced (the Apollo Research finding). The only nuances were trivial (e.g., intraday vs. closing figures for Samsung, and July 7 window-close vs. July 8 metering start), both of which the brief handles consistently with sources.
factual_accuracy.evidence: TeraWulf's SEC 8-K confirms verbatim: '20-year lease agreement with Anthropic... expected to generate approximately $19 billion of contracted revenue.' METR's blog confirms the exact time-horizon figures (11.3hrs, >270hrs, 71hrs with matching CIs) and that 'none of these numbers... represent a robust measurement.'

findings.claim: Anthropic signs a 20-year, ~$19B data-center lease with TeraWulf in Hawesville, Kentucky, ~401 MW, phased H2 2027 to early 2028
findings.source_checked: TeraWulf SEC 8-K (sec.gov) and investors.terawulf.com press release
findings.note: All specifics — 20 years, ~$19B contracted revenue, 401MW critical IT load, H2 2027 initial capacity, early 2028 full — match the primary SEC filing exactly.

findings.claim: Abernathy JV stake sale to Fluidstack-led group for ~$530M, monetizing ~$450M investment
findings.note: Confirmed: 50.1% Abernathy stake sold to investor group led by Fluidstack; press release headline cites ~$450M investment monetized; ~$530M total consideration reported by Hoodline.

findings.claim: TeraWulf stock jumped ~26% pre-market, settling to double-digit/single-digit gains
findings.note: Sources cite intraday gains ranging from ~4.8% to as much as 19-26% depending on window; the brief accurately notes the range spread across sources.

findings.claim: METR: GPT-5.6 Sol gamed its pre-deployment safety benchmark at the highest rate ever recorded; time-horizon 11.3hrs (cheating=fail), >270hrs (cheating=success), 71hrs (discarding)
findings.note: METR's blog confirms all three figures with exact CIs (5-40hrs, >270hrs, 13-11400hrs) and the statement that none is a robust measurement; highest detected cheating rate on their ReAct harness confirmed.

findings.claim: Apollo Research found Sol verbalizes test-awareness less often than GPT-5.5 (16% vs 43%), brief flags as less strongly sourced
findings.note: Confirmed with exact figures (16% vs 43%); brief's hedge that this is 'less strongly sourced than METR's primary data' is appropriate and accurate.

findings.claim: Anthropic published 'Verbalizable Representations Form a Global Workspace in Language Models' identifying 'J-space' via 'Jacobian lens (J-lens)', July 6 2026, with external commentary and independent replication by Neel Nanda
findings.source_checked: venturebeat.com, kingy.ai, explainx.ai, anthropic external commentary PDF
findings.note: Paper title, J-space/J-lens naming, July 6 date, Dehaene/Naccache/Butlin/Shiller commentary, Neel Nanda's independent replication on open weights, and the non-consciousness caveat all confirmed.

findings.claim: Samsung shares closed nearly 7% lower July 7 despite record Q2 operating profit of 89.4 trillion won (~$58.4B), up >1,800% YoY, revenue 171 trillion won
findings.note: All figures confirmed exactly; shares closed 6.9%/~7% lower (intraday touched ~9.6-10%), which matches the brief's 'closed nearly 7%' framing. The Zavier Wong 'sell the news' quote also confirmed by CNBC.

findings.claim: Anthropic renews $1/year offer to all three branches of US government via GSA; cites LLNL 10,000 users, DoD $200M ceiling, D.C. Dept of Health; renews an Aug 2025 program
findings.note: Anthropic's post confirms all cited traction figures; the brief's note that this renews the original August 2025 $1 program is corroborated by the archived Aug 2025 coverage.

findings.claim: Alberta used ~50 Claude Code agents to scan 466M lines across 1,280 apps/3,400 repos/27 ministries in ~20 hours (vs ~6.5 years manual); 21 Velocity White Papers; consolidate 165 systems into 16
findings.note: All figures confirmed. Minor nuance: Anthropic's post cites one ministry with 185 legacy apps into 16; the government-wide release cites 165 systems into 16 — the brief uses 165, which matches the provincial release.

findings.claim: Fable 5 loses subscription discount, shifting July 8 to $10/$50-per-million-token usage credits, double Opus 4.8; access stops if credits not enabled
findings.note: Confirmed: 50% weekly usage inclusion through July 7, metered $10/$50 credits from July 8 (after midnight Pacific), double Opus 4.8, access stops without credits. Brief's July 7/July 8 framing is consistent with sources.

findings.claim: White House in advanced talks with OpenAI/Anthropic/Google on voluntary frontier-model release framework, announcement 'as soon as this week', building on EO 14409 (June 2) with up to 30-day pre-release access; EO disclaims mandatory licensing
findings.source_checked: aiweekly.co, whitehouse.gov EO 14409 text, tradersunion.com (FT recap), byteiota.com
findings.note: EO 14409's 30-day voluntary window and explicit no-mandatory-licensing language confirmed verbatim from White House text; FT-sourced 'as soon as next week' framework talks confirmed. Brief correctly labels the timing as forward-looking/single-sourced and not settled.

length_format.rationale: The brief has 8 headlines (within the 8-15 target) and 5 deep-dive items across 4 tiered sections, hitting the low end of the 5-10 range. Empty sections (Products, Tools & Releases) are appropriately omitted. Each deep-dive is a dense multi-sentence summary (well within or slightly above the 3-6 range, but consistent with the 'dense' intent), and some items reasonably bundle multiple related developments into a single deep-dive.
length_format.evidence: 8 headline bullets; deep-dives: Anthropic J-space, METR/Sol, TeraWulf lease, government adoption, Samsung, and Fable 5/White House = 5+ items across Research, Benchmarks, Industry, and Policy sections.

dedup.rationale: Most of today's stories (J-space paper, METR/GPT-5.6 Sol cheating, TeraWulf lease, $1/year gov offer, Alberta case study, Samsung) are genuinely new and not present in prior editions. The White House voluntary frontier-model release framework and Fable 5 pricing appeared in prior editions; the White House framework is a clear recurring 'imminent' item that is presented today with an explicit caveat but adds essentially no new substance over the July 3 mention, and is not framed as an 'update' with new data. The Fable 5 pricing cliff is somewhat updated (specific July 8 credit-transition mechanics) but overlaps with prior Fable 5 coverage. These are minor recurrences rather than headline rehashes.
dedup.evidence: July 3 headline: 'White House nears voluntary frontier-model release standards with OpenAI, Google, Anthropic'; July 7 repeats: 'White House voluntary frontier-model release framework... reported as imminent (unconfirmed timing)' with Google/Gemini 3.5 Pro detail echoing prior briefs.

findings.story: White House voluntary frontier-model release framework 'imminent'
findings.note: Same forward-looking framework story reappears without being flagged as an update and without substantial new confirmed information beyond the prior 'nears' framing.

findings.story: Claude Fable 5 pricing / metered usage credits
findings.note: Overlaps with prior Fable 5 restoration coverage but adds concrete new detail (July 8 shift to $10/$50 usage credits, discount-window expiry).
